title: CORS error when fetching images from CDN
symptom: When attempting to download images from a CDN, the fetch request fails with a CORS policy error indicating that no 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header is present.
applies_when: CORS policy is enforced by the browser
not_this_if: Images are displayed correctly in img tags
CORS headers are correctly configured on the server
root_cause: The CORS error occurs when the server does not include the 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header in its response, which is required for cross-origin requests. This can also be affected by browser caching, which may serve outdated responses without the necessary headers.
fix_steps: Disable caching in the browser's developer tools
Add a no caching header to your requests to prevent future issues
agent_instruction: Disable caching in the browser's dev tools and add a no caching header to your fetch requests.
